title: Patek Philippe Ref 783 “Montres Golf” Retailed By Gübelin Designed by Gilbert Albert w/ Pouch & Extract Unpolished
description: The watch is uniquely stylish and it is quite apparent when looking at certain elements, such as the segmented dial, that it was created by the designer. The watch is powered by the calibre 12-400, and the case measures 39mm in diameter and 8mm in height (including the crystal). In addition, it is a double-signed dial sold by the Lucerne-based retailer Gübelin. The case back features the Poinçon de Maître key numbered 2 indicating F. Baumgartner SA, a case specialist who produced the cases many water-resistant Calatrava wristwatches, including the legendary reference 2526. While many collectors prioritize wristwatches, this pocket watch is a treasure and an absolutely mesmerizing creation from one of the watch industry’s most talented minds. This particular model is from the “Montres Golf” collection of three different models depicted in the advertisement (reference 782, reference 783, and reference 799) with integrated chains made for golfers to have in their pocket when playing golf. These golf watches became somewhat in vogue in the mid-20th century as golf became more popular and it became more widely-known that the impact of a swing could damage a wristwatch’s movement. The integrated chain allowed the golfer to clip it to their pocket for quick access to the time when on the course and also to ensure that it would not accidentally get lost. The “Montres Golf” watches also frequently came with a brown leather pouch, sometimes embossed with PATEK PHILIPPE in gold, but in the case of this watch embossed with GÜBELIN in gold. This reference 783 has an ornate wreath-like pattern on the chain and edge of the case that makes it particularly appealing. This watch came from the grandson of the original owner, who was a diplomat for Iran in the 1950s and 1960s, before the Shah was deposed, and likely was a gift to him by another diplomat. title: Richard Mille RM 002-V2 Tourbillon in 18K Rose Gold w/ Certificate of Authenticity & Box
description: The manufacturer is one of the most innovative manufacturers known for their use of lightweight and durable case materials most notably carbon TPT and titanium. Their distinct design language utilizes the tonneau case design with open work movements. The Richard Mille RM 002 is one of the earliest watch models from the manufacturer. This RM 002-V2 is an incredible example in 18k rose gold. It features a manual-wind tourbillon. The case measures 38mm in diameter by 45mm in length. These watches are incredibly innovative in that the crown’s button allows the wearer to operate the watch through different gears W (Winding), N (Neutral), and H (Hands/Time Setting). The RM 002-V2 is one of the best values in Richard Mille in that these trade significantly below the debut Richard Mille RM 001 which have sold as high as $2.2M at auction despite being essentially the same watch with the RM 001’s movement being made in collaboration with APRP (Audemars Piguet Renaud & Papi). The watch was serviced in December 2025 by Richard Mille for approximately $15,000 and is running well. It. It does come with a Certificate of Authenticity from Richard Mille and the RM box. It is the best price in the world.

title: Rolex Porcelain Dial Zenith Daytona Reference 16520 w/ Box Unpolished
description: This is a super attractive Zenith Daytona that features the highly-coveted “Porcelain” dial found in the earliest Zenith Daytonas! The “Porcelain” dial Daytonas are extremely rare and known for their unique design which are not made of porcelain as the nickname suggests. These dials are in fact a layered lacquer with incredible depth and feature the notable floating “COSMOGRAPH” font. This example features the early bezel variant. These watches are among the most expensive of the Zenith Daytonas and at one point were trading for over $200,000. The watch features an R9 serial dating to circa 1989. The case appears to be unpolished. It is powered by the automatic movement (Caliber 4030 made by Zenith), and is 40mm in diameter. It features the early matte finish Rolex 78360 bracelet without the flip lock with M date code dating to 1988. With the newly released enamel dial Daytona collectors have been very excited with the Porcelain dial Daytona as it appears to be the inspiration to the latest release. The watch does not include the papers. It does come with box. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: F.P.Journe Centigraphe Sport Titanium Reference CTS on Strap w Box & Booklet
description: The Centigraphe Sport CTS was produced from 2014 to 2018. Collectors love the Centigraphe Sport for the sport-oriented design with incredible versatility. This example is paired on a rubber strap which offers a more casual aesthetic for passionate F.P.Journe collectors. The case is made of titanium and measures approximately 42mm in diameter. The case has some wear on the bezel at 10 o'clock. The movement is an engineering highlight. It is powered by the calibre 1506 - a movement which is made of titanium and weighs approximately 12 grams. The dial is made of aluminum alloy and sapphire featuring luminous numerals. The watch is arguably one of the most advanced chronograph models ever produced capable of timing one-hundredth of a second. The subdials feature a 1/100th of a second at 10 o'clock, a 20 second dial, and a 10-minute dial at 6 o'clock. Titanium Centigraphes purportedly made 50% less than aluminium examples. We have been told that only 10 examples in this titanium configuration were made, however we are not certain of this. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with papers. It comes with a box, usb drive and CD with booklet and cloth. We do not know the date of production.

title: F.P.Journe Octa Automatique Lune Havana 40mm Full Set As New
description: The Octa Automatique Lune Havana edition was introduced in circa 2016 and produced until 2018. This F.P.Journe Octa Automatique Lune Havana in 18k rose gold measures approximately 40mm in diameter. The hands are a very beautiful ivory color steel. The moon disc is incredibly beautiful. The watch is in as new condition. The watch is powered by the calibre 1300.3. It features a 5-day power reserve. This watch comes with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch dates to 2020. For those looking for an outstanding F.P. Journe, this Lune is a special timepiece.

title: F.P.Journe Classique Chronometre Souverain 40mm White Dial in 18K RG Unpolished Full Set
description: This is a beautiful example dating to 2020. It features the stunning “smooth” silver dial with radial Breguet numerals. The case is made of rose gold. It measures 40mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. It features some wear near 4 o'clock. It is paired on a green F.P.Journe strap. It is powered by the calibre 1304. It comes full set with box and papers from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: F.P.Journe Chronometre Souverain Dubai Limited Edition in Platinum Full Set
description: This example is a limited edition of 99 examples celebrating the grand opening of F.P.Journe’s first boutique in Dubai in 2019. It comes from the original owner. It features the beautiful green dial with radial Breguet numerals. The case is made of platinum, and measures 40mm in diameter. The case is unpolished. The strap has some wear. It is powered by the calibre 1304. It comes full set with box and papers. It is running well. This example was purchased in 2021. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Perpetual Calendar 2657OR.OO.12200R.02 in 18K RG w/ Full Set Unpolished
description: The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is an icon. It is the original luxury sports watch that created and defined the category. Introduced at BaselWorld in 1972 after being designed by Gérald Genta, it turned the Swiss industry upside down and changed the fortunes of the struggling Audemars Piguet. It’s no surprise, that Patek Philippe hired Gerald Genta a few years later to introduce their own version of a steel luxury sports watch: the Nautilus. This Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Perpetual Calendar reference 2657OR.OO.12200R.02 is an incredible perpetual calendar. It features a blue “Grande Tapisserie” dial. The rose gold case measures 9.5mm in height and 41mm in diameter. The case is in great condition and appears unpolished. The watch is powered by the calibre 5134 an incredible complicated movement made by Audemars Piguet. The dial on this model is insanely attractive. The moon disc is one of the most beautiful examples in Audemars’ catalog. For those looking for a grail complicated Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, this 2657OR.OO.12200R.02 is an excellent choice. The watch comes with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. We believe the watch dates to 2017.

title: Rolex Ice Blue Daytona Reference 126576TBR in Platinum w/ Diamond Bezel Full Set Stickered New
description: This is an incredibly beautiful Rolex Daytona reference 126576TBR. The watch is made of platinum and features the “Ice Blue” dial that collectors love. It is an off-catalog Daytona with brilliant diamond setting. These are among the most important offerings from the manufacturer and made in very limited production due to the extreme intricacy of gem setting. No other company creates gem set watches like Rolex. This is an internal artisanal department within Rolex which utilizes the highest-quality flawless diamonds which are hand-set by master jewelers. These watches are incredibly special from the manufacturer in that they require the most hand intervention with a zero-tolerance for error. The dial features the factory baguette-cut and diamond hour markers. It features an immaculate diamond-set bezel. It is powered by the fantastic calibre 4131 which is on display with the exhibition caseback. The case measures 40mm in diameter. It is stickered and in new condition. For those looking for a luxurious sports Rolex watch, it is hard to beat the Daytona Reference 126576TBR! The watch dates to late 2024. The watch is running well. It comes with a box or papers from the original owner.

title: Rolex Tiffany & Co. Daytona Reference 6263
description: The present watch is a Daytona we have been chasing from the original owner for a long time. The original owner received this Daytona reference 6263 as a wedding present in 1973 from his in-laws just as he was starting medical school. His in-laws purchased the watch brand new from the prestigious retailer Tiffany & Co. It is a special example featuring the highly-coveted double-signed dial. It has been a faithful timekeeper for him for several decades. At one point a patient commented on the value and rarity of the watch, which surprised the original owner! Tiffany-signed watches from Rolex and Patek Philippe are among the most desirable category in vintage watches. The Rolex Daytona reference 6263 was introduced in circa 1969 and remained in production until circa 1988. This Daytona is fresh to market. On the caseback is his initials which were engraved at Tiffany & Co. The case has been lightly polished during a Rolex service in the past. The caseback appears to be unpolished. This example features a 3.59M case serial dating to circa 1973. It measures 37mm in diameter. It features the original Mk I bezel that is worth approximately $20,000-$25,000 by itself. It is paired on a Rolex C&I Oyster rivet bracelet which was produced in United States dating to circa 1974. The luminous plots on the dial appear to have been relumed in the past. It features Mk 3 service pushers. The crown has been replaced. The watch does not come with box and papers. It was serviced by Rolex in 2012. it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Day-Date United States Merchant Marine Reference 18038 Unpolished
description: The Rolex Day-Date was introduced in 1956 and was the first watch to display the date, as well as the fully-spelled-out day of the week. This Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 is an incredibly attractive watch with an unbelievably rare United States Merchant Marine dial. This particularly with this original non-Rolex gold bracelet makes for a seriously impressive watch. We would rather have this logo on our watch than the signature of a despot who allegedly committed patricide, honestly! The 18K-gold case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). This particular example dates to circa 1986 with a 9.31M serial. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. For those looking for a prolific vintage Day-Date, this is one of the coolest examples we have offered!

title: Rolex Submariner Reference 5514
description: The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic watches of all time. We are excited to present this very unique—and possibly one-off— Submariner reference 5514 for sale. This watch was previously owned by watchmaker Dr. Greg Petronzi of True Patina. When the watch first arrived in Greg’s possession, he questioned its originality. The Submariner reference 5514—a special model manufactured by Rolex for the French dive company COMEX—was previously thought to have been produced only in the mid-1970s, primarily within the 5 million serial range. This particular example, however, bears a serial number in the 7 million range, dating it to 1982. As Greg opened the watch to service it, the story became even more intriguing. The movement was not a cal. 1520 (the expected movement for a “2-line” Submariner), but rather a serialized, chronometer-spec cal. 1570. At the time, these movements were typically found only in “4-line” Submariners such as the 5512 or 1680. Furthermore, the movement plate had a small section removed to accommodate the Helium Escape Valve (HEV) characteristic of the 5514. As Greg noted, this resection appeared to be a factory-level modification. It was also observed that the inside of the caseback had been routed in the area where “Patented” was originally engraved. This is consistent with watches from the early 1980s, when the Oyster case patent expired and “Registered Design” markings between the lugs were changed to “Orig. Rolex Design” (as seen on this example). This detail further supports that the caseback is correctly matched to the mid-case for the period. The Mk4 Maxi dial is correct for a 1982 production watch. Given the consistency across the dial, mid-case, and caseback, all components appear to be original. The hands have been color matched. The case has been polished. Ultimately, only Rolex can definitively verify originality. The chronometer-grade cal. 1570 is serialized (unlike the 1520), and Rolex alone maintains records correlating moveme

title: Rolex GMT-Master Ref. 1675 Tiffany & Co.
description: This Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 is a rare double-signed timepiece sold by the New York retailer. The GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan-Am pilots in the 1950s, and remains one of the most sought-after models in the brand’s lineup. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it is still a flagship model for Rolex to this day. This Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 is a collector favorite reference in recent years. The case features a 2.009 million serial number, this example dates to circa 1969. It comes with an American made Oyster bracelet dating to 1968. The hands, and possibly the dial have been re-lumed. This has been polished in the past. The insert is slightly newer. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article. The watch does not come with a box or papers. The watch was serviced in 2025 by Rolliworks and is running well. It comes with service papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex GMT-Master 1675 w/ Box and Papers
description: The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. This example features an attractive warm patina on the luminous matte dial. The bezel insert has faded to faint light pink. The case features a 5.5M serial dating to circa 1978. The bracelet and bezel assembly are polished. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired with a reference 78350 Oyster bracelet with 580 end links. The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. It was originally sold in 1978. It was serviced in 2024 and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel ’.

title: Rolex GMT-Master II Pepsi 126710BLRO on Jubilee Full Set Unpolished Mk 1 Bezel
description: The Rolex “Pepsi” GMT-Master II reference 126710BLRO is one of the most sought-after modern sports watches today. It captures the iconic DNA from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day. This example is in great condition. It features the Mark 1 bezel which has a more vivid blue. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter. The watch was originally sold in 2019. It comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article.

title: Rolex Meteorite Cosmograph Daytona 126519LN in 18K WG on Oysterflex Full Set Unpolished
description: The Rolex Daytona Cosmograph reference 126519LN with Meteorite dial is a fantastic iteration of the iconic sports watch. It is paired on its original Oysterflex band suited to be worn in all active situations. The watch features a Meteorite dial, black Cerachrom bezel, black registers and the beautiful red-arched “Daytona” font. The case is in 18K white gold. The case appears to be unpolished. It features a D and F strap length. It comes “full set” with original box and papers and was sold in 2026.

title: Rolex Domino's Air-King 14000 w/ Papers Unpolished
description: The Rolex-Domino’s connection is perhaps the ultimate high-low collaboration. These watches were not made for purchase by the public, but as a way to reward Domino’s franchise owners who hit their sales targets. All these watches were originally ordered through Schlanderer and Sons in Ann Arbor, Michigan, where Domino’s has its headquarters. In the past, these watches on the secondhand market were generally the least expensive Rolex a person could purchase as few people wanted a pizza logo like this on their dial. Dealers would buy them, swap out the Domino’s dial, and polish off the case back initials. Over the last year, however, they have gained a massive following. This example dates to circa 1996 with an T5 serial. The dial is incredibly attractive. The case appears to be unpolished. It does not come with box. It does come with papers. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. You can learn about these watches in our video Collector Conversation: Tom Monaghan - Founder of Domino's Pizza - on the Domino's Rolex . To learn more about Rolex-Domino’s story, watch the video ”Rolex Model NEVER in Stores” .

title: Rolex 26mm Bark Datejust Reference 6927 in 18K YG Unpolished
description: This Rolex Datejust reference 6927 is a very special example in 18k yellow gold with bark textured bezel and bracelet. The case measures 26mm in diameter. The 26mm Datejust is an incredibly chic timepiece which has become one of the most desired ladies wristwatches in recent years. The watch dates to circa 1978 and features an 5.41M serial. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on the original Rolex Presidential bracelet which are incredibly hard to find. The watch does not come with box, but comes with papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Reference 67198 on Ladies Jubilee Link in 18K YG Unpolished
description: This Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 67198 is a very special example in 18k yellow gold. The watch dates to circa 1981 and features an 6.7M serial. The case measures 26mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired on the original Rolex ladies Jubilee bracelet which are incredibly hard to find. The watch does not come with box, but comes with papers. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Frog Foot Mk 1 Dial Explorer I Reference 1016 Unpolished
description: Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. This example features an 1.73M serial dating it to circa 1967. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It features the amazing Mark 1 frog foot dial. It features “fat” style lume. It is paired on a Rolex 7026 Rolex bracelet with 58 end links dating to 1966. The bracelet has been lightly polished and was added later. For those looking for an awesome matte-dial Explorer, look no further. To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I . The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Gilt Explorer I Ref 1016 on Folded Link Bracelet
description: The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. This example dates to circa late 1967 with a 1.587M serial. The case has been polished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on Rolex folded Oyster 7836 bracelet with incorrect 280 end links dating to circa 1972. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers. It recently passed a 3 bar water resistance test. To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE :

title: Rolex Explorer I Silver Font Gilt Dial Reference 1016 Unpolished
description: Introduced in 1960 an icon was born in the Rolex Explorer reference 1016. The 1016 was produced until circa 1989. The ref. 1016 marked the first Explorer with “Superlative Chronometer Officially Certified" printed on the dial. It is one of the most desired timepieces and for great reason. The 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design and for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. This example features a beautiful gilt dial with the very rare silver font. It features a 517,XXX serial dating it to circa 1960. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). The lume is very attractive. It is paired on a Jubilee bracelet dated to 1960. For those looking for an amazing gilt-dial 1016, this is an awesome watch. To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see my video and corresponding article Reference Points A Comprehensive Collector's Guide To The Rolex Explorer I . title: Rolex Day-Date Ref 118339 with Diamond Lugs and Sapphire Indices in White Gold w/ Card
description: This Rolex Day-Date reference 118339 is an incredibly timepiece. The case is 18K white gold with spectacular factory diamond set lugs. The dial features sapphire indices at 6 and 9 o’clock. It comes on a white gold President bracelet. The watch does not come with a box. It comes with its card, and was originally sold in Hong Kong in 2014. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Pre-Daytona Reference 6234
description: This Rolex Pre-Daytona reference 6234 is an awesome and uncommon Rolex chronograph. The watch dates to circa 1961 with a 688,XXX serial number. It is paired on a Rolex stretch Rivet bracelet with 57 end links dating to 1962. It features a great blue telemeter scale with arrowhead hour markers. The case has been polished in the past. The stainless steel case measures approximately 36mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). Pre-Daytona models have always been a great value in vintage Rolex and are some of the most interesting chronograph models of the era. It features a TrueDome D21 replacement crystal. The watch does not come with box and papers. It was just serviced and is running well.

title: Rolex Yacht-Master Reference 116622-0002 Rolesium/Platinum Full Set
description: The Rolex Yacht-Master reference 116622-0002 is a very special timepiece. The case is made of Rolesium - the watchmaker’s patented combination of Oystersteel and platinum. The case measures 40mm in diameter. The watch comes full set with box and papers. The watch dates to 2014. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Precision Ref. 4411 In 14k Rose Gold Unpolished
description: This Rolex Precision is an extremely elegant timepiece in 14K rose gold. The case appears to be unpolished and measures 34mm in diameter (42mm lug-to-lug). The watch is in spectacular condition. It is an incredibly elegant case which features beautiful tear drop lugs. The watch features a bold dauphine handset, and a non-luminous dial. The watch is paired with a period correct 9K rose gold Rolex buckle with British hallmarks (that is probably $1,500 alone). The watch dates to the early 1950s. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

title: Rolex Matte Dial GMT-Master Ref. 16750
description: The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The Rolex GMT-Master reference 16750 was introduced in circa 1979 with a production run until circa 1988. These models feature the calibre 3075 which improved upon the ref. 1675’s movements with a higher vibrations per hour at 28,800 and quickset date function. The watch dates to circa 1981 with an 6.7M serial. The bezel has a striking blue and a very cool red. The case has been polished. The hands appear to have been relumed. It is paired on a Rolex Joske’s oval link made in Mexico bracelet. This watch does not come with a box and papers. The service history is unknown, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel ’.

title: Rolex GMT-Master Ref 16750 Spider Dial
description: The Rolex GMT-Master reference 16750 was introduced in circa 1979 with a production run until circa 1988. These models feature the calibre 3075 which improved upon the ref. 1675’s movements with a higher vibrations per hour at 28,800, hacking seconds and quickset date function. This example features an 8.5M serial and dates to circa 1984. The case is polished. The hands have been relumed. The watch presents well, with a desirable patina and attractive bezel fade. The dial is a spider dial. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master ' video and read the corresponding article. The watch does not come with box and papers. It recently passed a water resistance of 5 bar. It was recently serviced and is running well.

title: Rolex GMT-Master Reference 16700 w/ Black Insert Unpolished
description: This Rolex GMT-Master reference 16700 is a very attractive example. It dates to circa 1999 with an A serial. The case appears to be unpolished. The bracelet features an X date code and is likely original to the watch. It is a Swiss Only dial and holes case! To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master ' video and read the corresponding article. You can also read our comprehensive article on the earlier reference ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel ’. The watch does not come with box and papers. description: The most desirable models from the retailer would be the Seafarer. This reference 2443 is the first of two variants with the “psychedelic” tidal register in graduated colors. variant. It is much more similar to the Seafarer reference 346. The later version had more of a Tiffany blue color in the minute register and the tidal register was both that Tiffany blue color and yellow rather than graduated colors. All of these early Seafarer models are extremely rare. The watch features a Valjoux 72 movement which was modified to incorporate a tide complication. The case appears to be unpolished. This Seafarer has a steel case and measures in at 38mm wide. The bottom right lug is bent from an impact. The dial is weathered, but appears to be original. It is paired on a contemporary beads of rice bracelet. The lume in the hands has been redone. The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently services and is running well.

This is one of the last “Big Subs” First Execution dial Autavia reference 2446 examples made with a serial in the 53,XXX range and T / SWISS in the hour register indicating tritium lume. title: Audemars Piguet Royal Oak C Series Ref 5402ST w/ Box
description: The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is an icon. It is the original luxury sports watch that created and defined the category. Introduced at BaselWorld in 1972 after being designed by Gérald Genta, it turned the Swiss industry upside down and changed the fortunes of the struggling Audemars Piguet. It’s no surprise, that Patek Philippe hired Gerald Genta a few years later to introduce their own version of a steel luxury sports watch: the Nautilus. The origin of the species is the Royal Oak reference 5402ST - nicknamed the “Jumbo”. The dial is very attractive with a slight touch of warm patina on the surface. The watch should fit a wrist up slightly approximately 7 inches and features the original “AP” flip clasp found on C series examples. The C series models were began production in 1976 and were eventually sold unti circa 1987. This example dates to the later tail of production. The case measures 39mm in diameter (48mm lug to lug) and this is the original “Jumbo”. The case and bracelet have been lightly polished in the past like on the vast majority of these, but this case is still super attractive on the wrist. This watch does not come with papers. It does come with a period correct box. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

The origin of the species is the Royal Oak reference 5402ST - nicknamed the “Jumbo”. It is no surprise that the A series examples are hotly hunted and desired by collectors around the world. Only a few seem come to market each year. The hands appear to be service replacement or relumed. It features a service crown. This watch does not come with box and papers. The watch was recently serviced and is running well. For those looking for something eclectic, and highly interesting this is a great opportunity. Despite our passion leaning towards vintage watches, there are certainly a number of modern watches that are undeniably awesome. Urwerk watches represent forward thinking watchmaking and get just about everyone excited - whether you are simply curious about watches or are amazed at the linear format on the timepiece. This is truly a fun watch to wear. The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history of the watch but it is running well. It was previously sold at Phillips for the 2015 “Only Watch” charity auction in favour of research on Duchenne muscular dystrophy, and belonged to an incredible collector! It comes with the 2 year International Warranty paperwork from Urwerk dating to 2020.

title: Universal Geneve Compax Ref. 885103/01 with Nina Rindt Dial Unpolished
description: This is a Universal Geneve Compax reference 885103/01. It features a Nina Rindt dial which could possibly belong to a reference 885103/02. It is possible that dial was put in this case. The watch case appears to be unpolished. It has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and measures 36mm in diameter, and approximately 45mm lug-to-lug. It does not include the original box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Universal Geneve Compax with MK1 Nina Rindt Dial
description: This is a Universal Geneve Compax with the MK 1 Nina Rindt dial with applied U logo and thinner register hands. The reference and serial are not visible on the case back. The case has been polished. It comes from the family of the original owner. It has a manual-wind movement (Caliber 72 made by Valjoux), and measures 36mm in diameter, and approximately 45mm lug-to-lug. It does not include the original box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Breguet Classique Quantième Perpétuel 7327 7327BB/11/9VU Perpetual Calendar w/ Papers
description: This Breguet Classique is a beautiful perpetual calendar wristwatch. The watch is powered by the automatic calibre 502.3.P. The case is made of 18k white gold and measures 39mm in diameter. It features 20mm lug width. The case appears to be unpolished. This extraordinary timepiece retails for $109,100! It is a tremendous discount. For those looking for an incredible complicated dress watch, this is very special. It comes with papers from the original owner. It does not come with box. The watch was sold in October 2023 and is in fantastic condition.

title: Gronefeld 1969 DeltaWorks in Stainless Steel Full Set Brand New
description: This Gronefeld 1969 DeltaWorks is a great ‘sports utility’ watch in stainless from the Dutch independent watchmakers. The watch is inspired by the Dutch Delta Works and designed to cope with the perils of water ingress and shocks while still upholding the elevated standards of horology and traditional craftsmanship. The case measures 45mm in diameter and is 12.5mm thick. The watch features an exhibition caseback displaying the calibre G-06 movement. This example features the ‘Khaki’ dial. The watch is in great shape and appears to be unpolished. This watch comes with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch dates to 2026.

title: Cartier Tank Asymétrique WGTA0043 in 18k Rose Gold Unpolished w/ Box and Papers
description: This Cartier Tank Asymétrique reference WGTA0043 is an elegant time only Rose Gold watch. The watch features the iconic diagonal case and dial design. It is a limited edition of 100 examples. The case measures approximately 44.5x 26.5mm at the longest points. It features a grey dial with white printed numerals. The watch is manually wound. It comes with a crocodile leather Cartier strap and 18k rose gold pin buckle. The watch was introduced in 2020. This example was sold in 2020. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

title: Longines Swissair 24-Hour Reference 6630 w/ Extract from the Archives
description: This Longines is an incredibly interesitng and rare oversized wristwatch made for Swissair pilots. The stainless steel case is a massive 47mm in diameter and 57mm lug-to-lug. The watch displays a 24-hour dial. It features a spade hour hand and leaf minute hand. The watch features a 9mm onion crown. The watch was invoiced to Swissair in 1957. These watches have sold as high as $176,000 at auction. For those looking for incredible aviation Longines timepieces, these are special watches. The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It comes with Extract from the Archives. The watch is paired on the original strap and buckle appears to be original.

title: OMEGA Speedmaster Reference 145.022-69 BA In 18K YG
description: A Speedmaster Reference 145.022-69 BA in 18K YG OMEGA introduced the Speedmaster in 1957, initially created for drivers in automotive racing needing to time their laps. However, the Speedmaster would reach eternal iconic status by eventually being qualified for spaceflight by NASA after an intense testing of several of the highest-grade chronographs of the 1960s. NASA selected the Speedmaster as the exclusive watch for use by its astronauts. The OMEGA Speedmaster reference 145.022-69 BA was the first OMEGA Speedmaster to be produced in 18k yellow gold. Only 30 examples were produced in total for astronauts and two were produced for President Nixon and Vice President Agnew. The next examples were given to Astronauts and are considered by many to be the grail OMEGA Speedmasters. On October 20, 2022, Wally Schirra’s personal OMEGA Speedmaster Professional BA 145.022 was sold for $1,906,954 . This example is one that was sold to the public. The 18K yellow-gold case is in has been lightly polshed. The dial is fantastic and the bezel remains in good condition. The watch is powered by the caliber 861. The bracelet is long and should fit a wrist approximately 7.1 inches. Finding an example with a long bracelet is incredibly difficult. For those looking for an epic gold Speedmaster, these are amazing watches. To learn more about the OMEGA Speedmaster, you can watch my ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Entire Lineage Of The OMEGA Speedmaster ’ video and read the corresponding article. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do now know the service history, but it is running well. title: OMEGA Railmaster Reference 2914-4 SC Unpolished
description: A Railmaster Reference 2914-4 SC Unpolished This OMEGA Ralimaster reference 2914-4 SC is an awesome watch. It has very beautiful lume. We believe the case is unpolished but heavily worn. The case measures 38mm by 47mm lug to lug. The watch dates to circa 1959 with a 17.3M movement number. The crown is a replacement. This watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well.

title: Gallet USMC Non-Luminous Clamshell Chronograph
description: This Gallet chronograph is an awesome vintage watch from circa the 1940s. The clamshell cases on these chronographs are among my favorite designs and are the earliest waterprotected chronographs. This particular example is a very special watch that belonged to US Marine William Sargis, who was in the 3rd Marine Division. The watch was received by Sargis prior to the 3rd Division’s official origin in September 1942, when the division was sent to New Zealand for training. In 1943, the 3rd fought in the Battle of Bougainville, and in 1944 it fought in the Battle of Guam. The collector has spoken with Sargis’ son who confirmed that his father made it home from the war and passed away in 1979. The case has been lightly polished. The crown has been replaced. It measures approximately 34.5mm in diameter (42mm lug to lug). It features an attractive black non-luminous dial. It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Ikepod Hemipode Grand Date on Rare Bracelet Full Set Unpolished
description: This Ikepod Hemipode Grande Date is in new old stock condition and is COSC certified. The watch features a 43mm stainless steel case and a 4500 Dubois Depraz movement with a chronograph function. The watch features a black dial with an orange chronograph hand. It is is in outstanding condition and unpolished. The bracelet is rare and extremely comfortable on the wrist. It comes with full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Titus Big Crown Calypsomatic Ref. 7840 Unpolished
description: This Titus Calypsomatic reference 7840 is an extremely cool vintage dive watch. It is reminiscent of many Rolex Big Crown Submariners and there is a strong collector community for these watches. The watch features the nicest and most attractive glossy dial I have seen on any example. It features warm patina on the hands and markers - which are tritium. This is like wearing a Big Crown without radium! The stainless steel case measures approximately 38mm in diameter (48mm lug-to-lug). The watch comes with an original three link bracelet signed by Titus. For those looking for a world class Titus Big Crown Calypsomatic, this is one of the best! The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Rolex GMT-Master II Pepsi 126710BLRO on Jubilee Full Set Unpolished
description: A from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day. This example is in great condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It measures 40mm in diameter. The watch was originally sold in 2019 and comes from the original owner. It comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and read the corresponding article. title: Rolex Milgauss Ref. 116400 Black Dial Full Set Unpolished
description: This Rolex Milgauss reference 116400 features the black dial with orange accents and iconic thunderbolt hand. The dial is white with silvered numerals at 2,4, 8 and 10 o’clock which are very interesting and make a visual statement. The watch features an exhibition caseback displaying the movement and engraved rotor. It comes with a platinum folding AP clasp from Audemars Piguet. The case appears to be unpolished. It comes with a box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Rolex Flat Four Kermit Submariner Ref 16610 T Full Set
description: This Rolex Submariner “Kermit” Submariner Reference 16610 T is an awesome sports watch. It features the “Flat 4” insert which collectors love. The condition is outstanding. title: Vacheron Constantin Patrimony Semainier Reference 47052 in 18K White Gold Unpolished
description: This Vacheron Constantin Patrimony reference 47052 is an incredible timepiece. The case is made of 18K white gold. It measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. title: Universal Geneve “Evil Nina” Compax  Ref. 885103/01

title: Rolex IBM Datejust 16220 Full Set
description: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel ’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have and the simplicity in this silver-dial reference 16220 is so beautiful. The watch dates to circa 1991 and features an X serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a 78360 Rolex Oyster bracelet and 558 end links. The watch is in excellent condition. The caseback features the former owner’s name engraved with his employer’s (IBM) logo. It was received by the employee in 1991. The case has been lightly polished. Learn more by reading Gavin Galiardo’s Collector's Guide: The IBM Rolex Datejust . We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It comes with box and papers.

It measures 40mm in diameter and 47mm lug-to-lug. The lugs are compact and angled downward, which is isolating for those who prefer traditional sizes. The case appears to be unpolished. It is powered by the Valjoux calibre 723. The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running strong.

description: A Speedmaster Reference 2998-4 Unpolished w/ Extract The OMEGA Speedmaster is certainly one of the most iconic watches with a truly rich history of exploration and sport. title: Zenith El Primero Reference A385 on GF Bracelet
description: The Zenith El Primero sits amongst the most celebrated chronographs in watchmaking. Powered by the automatic high-beat calibre 3019, the highly emphasized red chronograph hand glides over the dial in the smoothest manner. The watch measures approximately 37mm wide (with a lug-to-lug length of 46.5mm). The lug width is 19mm. This particular example comes with the Gay Frères steel ladder bracelet. The case and bracelet have been polished. The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history is running well.

title: Rolex Matte Dial Submariner Reference 16800
description: The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This reference 16800 features a 6.4M serial number dating to circa 1980. The watch features the transitional matte dial and the tritium luminous material on the hour has taken a warm patina. It is paired on a later Rolex reference 93150 Oyster bracelet with U date code dating to circa 1998. The case and bracelet appear to have been polished in the past. The bezel insert features a distinct blue-grey faded patina. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and read the corresponding article. The watch does not come with a box or papers. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Lémania 5012 for South African Air Force
description: The Lemania 5012 SAAF (South African Air Force) is a very cool and rare model. These watches were issued in the 1980s and it is estimated that 800 examples were sent to the SAAF. The watch is powered by the Lemania calibre 5012 automatic chronograph. The case measures 41mm in diameter. The case appears to be lightly polished. The watch was recently serviced and is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

title: Breitling Chronograph Ref. 777 Gilt Dial Unpolished
description: This Breitling chronograph reference 777 is an awesome vintage chronograph in great condition. The gilt glossy dial is highly attractive and features a gold-colored minutes/seconds track and a copper-colored outer tachymeter track. The serial number places it into the circa late 1943 to early 1944 range. The dial has an attractive and small “ANTI-CHOC” signature above 6:00 for the anti-shock capabilities of the Venus movement. It is paired on a contemporary beads of rice bracelet. The pushers appear to be original but have lost the chrome plating. The case measures approximately 37mm in diameter. The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

description: A from the models created for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day. title: Heuer Monaco Reference 1533G on Bracelet Unpolished
description: The Heuer Monaco was introduced in 1969 with the first automatic chronograph movement. Coupled by the association of Steve McQueen’s decision to wear the watch in the film ‘Le Mans’, the watch is nothing short of iconic. This example is an awesome reference 1533G. The watch is in fantastic condition. The case appears to be unpolished. It features a rare solid metallic grey dial grey dial variant. It is paired on a “Mark 1” Heuer NSA (Novavit SA) bracelet. It is powered by the automatic calibre 15 with running seconds. For those looking for an outstanding vintage Monaco, this is a great option! The watch does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. It comes with a rally strap and Heuer signed buckle.

title: A. Lange & Söhne Saxonia Thin Ref. 201.027 in 18k White Gold Full Set
description: The case is made of 18k white gold, and measures approximately 37mm in diameter, 43mm lug to lug and is 5.9mm thick. The watch is time only and features a silver dial and an exhibition caseback displaying the L093.1 movement. This model is a great entry into the brand. The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. The watch dates to 2020. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The retail price for this watch is $27,000.

title: A. Lange & Söhne Odysseus Ref 363.068 / LSLS3633AD in 18K WG Unpolished Box & Papers
description: The watch is in tremendous condition with a stunning grey guilloche dial. It features a beautiful calendar layout which is highly legible. The case style was a very forward thinking design by Lange at the time of release. It is made of 18K white gold. The white gold case appears to be unpolished and measures 40.5mm in diameter and 11.1mm in height. It is powered by the calibre L155.1 with a 50 hour power reserve. It is paired on an ALS rubber strap with white gold buckle. We do not know the production date, as the papers do not show this information. It was sold pre 2023. The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great.

title: Vacheron Constantin Overseas Black Dial Ref. 4500V/110A-B483 Full Set Unpolished + Straps
description: This Vacheron Constantin Overseas reference X45AV483 is a great modern sports watch from one of the most renowned and historically significant manufacturers. The case and bracelet are made of stainless steel. The watch is in excellent condition and appears to be unpolished with minor signs of wear. The case measures 41mm in diameter. It is paired on the stainless steel integrated bracelet and it comes with a leather and rubber strap from Vacheron Constantin. The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers, and is from the original owner. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. The watch was originally sold in November of 2021.

title: Rolex Submariner Reference 126610LN Unpolished Box & Papers
description: The Rolex Submariner is one of the most versatile and adorned wristwatches. This particular example was purchased in 2022. The case measures 41mm in diameter. It appears to be unpolished. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and read the corresponding article. The watch comes “full set” with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex MK II “Red Sub” Submariner Reference 1680
description: This Rolex reference 1680 “Red Sub” is a great ‘date’ Submariner in stainless steel. The watch features a 2.3M serial number and dates to circa 1970. It is paired with a reference 9315 Rolex Patent Pending folded link Oyster bracelet dating to 1970. The Patent Pending extension is worth thousands of dollars by itself, but does not appear to be original to the bracelet as it should likely be on an early bracelet with removable 280 end links and this has integrated 380 end links. The dial is an attractive example with slightly tropical color. It features Submariner in red writing along with tritium luminous plots that have turned warm with patina. The case has been polished. The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and corresponding article.

title: Heuer USS Whale Carrera Reference 2447S Eggshell Dial
description: This annual watch prize tradition started in 1938 and continues to this day. L.Y. Spear, President of the Electric Boat Company (which became General Dynamics), started the Spear Foundation in 1942 with an endowment to fund the watch prize. The Spear Foundation allowed the shipmate to pick out his own watch from Mallove’s Jewelers in Groton, Connecticut. Brooks wisely selected a Carrera chronograph, but there was a catch. Navy regulations restricted external radioactive sources aboard nuclear-powered boats, so as to ensure accurate monitoring of radiation levels from propulsion systems. This watch had its tritium luminous material removed from dial and hands. An early dial was evidently selected for this purpose. This was possibly done on special order from Heuer, or by the retailer at the time of original sale. Brooks served a distinguished Navy career, including as Commander of the Sturgeon-class nuclear-powered attack submarine USS Whale (SSN-638) from 1975 to 1978, retiring with the rank of Captain. As a civilian, Brooks held important Senate-confirmed positions in government. He was appointed Ambassador to lead negotiation of two strategic arms reduction treaties between the U.S. and Russia, and served as U.S. Undersecretary of Energy for Nuclear Security, responsible for managing the National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA). The watch is powered by the Valjoux calibre 72. The case is lightly polished but very strong. The dial and hands had the original tritium luminous material removed. The signed crown is original. The watch is fitted on a black leather rally strap from Abel Court with a rare signed vintage “Sun” buckle. The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch will be accompanied by military ephemera personally owned by Ambassador Brooks – a framed photograph of USS Whale (SSN-638) during her sea trials, a heavy bronze insignia removed from the Whale and mounted on a wood plaque for presentation to Brooks, and selected books from Brooks’

title: Rolex Submariner Reference 5513 w/ Box & Receipt
description: This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is a very cool example. The watch features a 1.815M serial dating to circa 1968. The case has been lightly polished but is strong. It is paired on a C&I Rolex Rivet bracelet dating to 1970, which was possibly sold on the watch. The lume appears to be original under UV light. Learn more about these watches within Charlie Dunne’s article “ Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512 ’. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and corresponding article. This watch comes with box and the original receipt. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Movado Breguet Numerals M95 Chronograph Unpolished
description: This Movado chronograph features a stunning dial with Breguet numerals. The case measures approximately 35mm in diameter and 43mm lug to lug. The case appears to be unpolished. It features very attractive and uncommon applied-Breguet numerals. It is powered by the legendary calibre M95! It is paired on a contemporary beads of rice bracelet. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

title: Rolex Green Dial 41MM Oyster Perpetual Reference 134300 Full Set New
description: The Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 134300 is an awesome modern watch. It features a great green dial. People absolutely love it and were losing their minds over it when it was released. It has great proportions at 41mm in diameter. The smooth bezel gives it a slightly more dressy look. The case is in phenomenal condition and unpolished. It is new. The watch comes with box and papers. It was sold in April 2026. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Patek Philippe Reference 2526 in 18K YG w/ Extract, Box & Service Box/Papers
description: The Patek Philippe reference 2526 is an iconic watch and this is an incredibly attractive example in 18K yellow gold. The Patek Philippe Reference 2526 is renowned as the most attractive important of Patek Philippe’s automatic references. The watch dates to 1956 and was sold in the 1957 according to the Extract from the Archives. The case has been polished. The dial is very beautiful. The 2 o’clock hour marker is slightly askew. The case dimensions are 36mm in diameter and 41mm lug-to-lug. The crown is a service replacement. It was recently serviced by Patek Philippe. It is comes with an 18mm Patek Philippe leather strap and features gold Patek Philippe buckle. It is powered by the iconic self-winding movement from Patek Philippe, the calibre 12-600. It features the 2nd series dial. The watch comes with box. It does not come with papers. It does come with an Extract from the Archives, service papers and a service box. The service papers date to January 2026.

title: Tudor Submariner Ref 79090 Unpolished
description: This Tudor Submariner reference 79090 is an exceptional example. We believe the case is unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1989 (299,XXX serial). The case measures 40mm in diameter (47mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on a 9315 folded Oyster bracelet with 380B end links and N2 date code. The watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Patek Philippe Reference 5146G-010 w/ Service Receipts and Extract
description: The Patek Philippe reference 5146G-010 is an awesome annual calendar model. The model is now discontinued. This particular example is a beautiful 18K white gold case. The case measures 39mm in diameter. It features a beautiful grey dial. It is paired on a brand new Patek Philippe strap. The watch does not come with a box or papers. It does come with service receipts from Patek Philippe dating to 2026 and is running well. The watch dates to 2007.

title: Rolex Silver Datejust Ref. 16014 Unpolished
description: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel ’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This Datejust features a silver “soleil” dial. This watch dates back to 1979 with a 5.8M serial. It measures 36mm in diameter. The Rolex Jubilee 62510H bracelet features a B date code dating to 1977. It features 555 end links. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is keeping excellent time. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Heuer Carrera 1153 Test Dial
description: This Heuer Carrera reference 1153! It features a cool watch with the Roman numeral test dial. These dials were not born in the watches, but rather prototype options for Heuer that never made it to a retail option. The case measures 38mm in diameter. It has been polished in the past. For those looking for an uncommon Heuer Carrera, this is a fun alternative to the reference 1153. This watch does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It is paired on an unsigned strap and unsigned buckle.

title: Rolex Explorer I Reference 1016 on Folded Oyster Bracelet
description: The Rolex Explorer reference 1016 is one of the purest expressions of watch design, and it is for this reason that I am probably asked for vintage Explorer reference 1016s more than any other watch. This example dates to circa late 1974 with a 3.6M serial. It features a tritium matte dial. The case has been polished. It measures 36mm in diameter (43mm lug-to-lug). It is paired on Rolex folded Oyster 7838 bracelet with 358 end links without date code dating to 1973-1975. The hands have been relumed. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It does not come with a box or papers. It recently passed a 5 bar water resistance test. To learn more about the history and evolution of the Rolex Explorer I, you can see the video and corresponding article I produced with HODINKEE :

title: Rolex Two-Tone Datejust Ref 16013 Unpolished
description: This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features a great champagne dial. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1986 with a 9.2M serial. The Rolex 62523 H Jubilee bracelet features a 08 date code dating to 1990. This watch does not include the box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running great! It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Transitional Matte Dial Submariner Reference 16800 Unpolished
description: The Rolex Submariner is one of the most renowned sports watches of all time. This reference 16800 features a 7.4M serial number dating to circa 1983. The case appears to be well worn, but unpolished. The luminous material on the dial has a nice warm color. It is paired on a Rolex Oyster bracelet reference 93150 with G date code dating to circa 1982. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and read the corresponding article. The watch does not come with a box or papers, we do not know the service history but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex GMT-Master 1675 Full Set
description: The Rolex GMT-Master was originally designed for Pan Am pilots in the 1950s and continues to be a flagship model for Rolex to this day. The reference 1675 was introduced in 1959 and remained in production until 1980. Originally designed for Pan-Am pilots in the 1950s, the Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most sought-after models in the brand’s lineup. Due to its incredible heritage and iconic look, it remains a flagship model for Rolex to this day. This Rolex GMT-Master reference 1675 is a collector favorite reference in recent years. With a 1.76 million serial number, this example dates to circa 1968. It comes with a C&I stretch rivet bracelet that dates to 1968. The case has been polished in the past. The bezel features an attractive fade, with the red turning pink and the outer of the blue bezel slightly white. The watch comes with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master you can watch the ‘Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master' video and corresponding article I produced. You can also read our comprehensive article ‘ Collector's Guide: The Rolex GMT-Master Reference 1675 In Steel ’.

title: Rolex GMT-Master II Coke Reference 16710 w/ CPO Card
description: Originally designed for Pan-Am pilots in the 1950s, the Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most sought-after models in the brand’s lineup. To learn more about the Rolex GMT-Master, you can watch my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex GMT-Master ' video and read the corresponding article.

title: Rolex Submariner Ref. 5513 w/ Box and Hang Tag

title: Rolex "Pre-Daytona" Chronograph Ref. 6238

title: Rolex "Bluesy" Submariner Ref. 16613 w/ Papers and Hangtag Unpolished

The case has been lightly polished. The dial is a silver tachymeter dial that is extremely attractive. It measures 36mm in diameter. This watch does not come with box and papers. title: Ebel 1911 Chronograph Ref. 8134901 Blue Dial 18k Yellow Gold

It features a stunning blue dial with applied roman numeral indexes. It is powered byt the Zenith El Primero movement. The serial on the interior suggests this was among the original Zenith El Primero movements that were allegedly stored away during the struggling years of the manufacturer. The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

In certain light it appears indigo. The watch measures approximately 36mm in diameter but wears slightly larger due to the lug profile. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1978 with a 5.5M serial number. It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. title: Rolex Datejust Ref. 16014 Linen Dial
description: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel ’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This reference 16014 with a linen dial is so beautiful. The watch comes on a 62510H Jubilee bracelet with a “G” clasp code, dating to circa 1982. It comes with the correct 555 end-links. It does not come with a box or papers.
