title: Patek Philippe Reference 5035P-024 in Platinum w/ Full Set
description: This Patek Philippe reference 5035P-024 is a great complicated timepiece with a luminous cream dial and platinum case. The watch is powered by the calibre 315 S QA 24H. The platinum case measures approximately 37mm in diameter (45mm lug-to-lug). These watches are a tremendous value for annual calendar Patek Philippe wristwatches. It is paired on a Patek Philippe leather strap and platinum buckle. The case is possibly unpolished, but with wear and scratches. The watch comes with a box and papers. It was originally sold in 2003. We do not know the service history of this watch, but it is running great.

title: Gallet “Jim Clark” Chronograph Unpolished
description: This Gallet chronograph in stainless steel is a stunning stainless steel sports chronograph. It features a black dial with tritium luminous material, and a printed tachymeter and telemeter scale. The stainless steel case measures 38mm in diameter and approximately 44m lug-to-lug. The watch earns its nickname after being worn by the Formula 1 legend Jim Clark. It is powered by the Excelsior Park 40. The gilt dial is very attractive. It dates to the mid 1960s. The watch does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

title: Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Reference 4100ST
description: The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is among the most iconic wristwatches. This example, a reference 4100ST, is a great compact Royal Oak. It measures approximately 35.5mm in diameter, but wears larger as it has a lug to lug measurement of 44mm. The bracelet can fit a wrist of approximately 7.25 inches. The case has been polished in the past. It features a beautiful blue dial. It does not come with a box or papers. We do not know the date of production. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Rolex Submariner "Meters First" Reference 5512
description: This Rolex Submariner reference 5512 is an excellent example featuring the “Meters First” dial which was famously worn by Steve McQueen. The watch is paired on what is likely its original C+I bracelet made for Rolex dating to 1970. The crystal has been replaced to now feature the True Dome D19. The case is very strong, and it has probably been lightly polished. The watch dates to circa 1968 with a 1.8M serial. It features the original “Long 5” insert. According to Rolex, the Submariner reference 5512’s production consisted of only 17,338 examples. The Submariner reference 5513’s production consisted of a much larger amount, 151,449 examples! Both Eric and I were quite astonished at the number of 5512 Submariners produced being a mere 17,338, as we assumed it was much larger - in the tens of thousands. In simplified terms, there was only just about one 5512 produced for every ten 5513s produced (or 11.4% specifically). Learn more about the watch within Charlie Dunne’s article “ Collector’s Guide: Understanding The Rolex Submariner Reference 5512 ’. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and corresponding article. The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Patek Philippe “Tasti Tondi” Reference 1463 in 18k Yellow Gold w/ Extract from the Archives
description: The Patek Philippe chronograph reference 1463 is one of the most important and iconic references from the manufacturer. It is estimated that 750 examples were produced. The reference 1463 was introduced in circa 1940 and remained in production until the 1960s. We are excited to share this incredible example in yellow gold dating to the 1950s. The two-register chronograph is powered by the calibre 13-130. These cases were produced by François Borgel (Taubert Frères). Taubert Frères is regarded as one of the most exceptional chronograph case manufacturers of all time. The reference 1463 was an innovative and sport-oriented chronograph which was the first waterproof chronograph from Patek Philippe. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. It measures approximately 35mm in diameter and 42.5mm lug-to-lug. The case appears to be lightly polished, but is very strong. It features an Arabic numeral dial which is very beautiful. The dial and hands are in excellent condition. Finding a grail reference such as this is a special opportunity. The watch is paired on a Honey Wind leather strap. It does not come with a box or papers. It comes with an Extract from the Archives. It was produced in 1953 and sold in 1954. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. For those with a passion for important Patek Philippe models, this is a particularly special timepiece.

title: Rolex John Mayer Daytona Reference 116508  in 18K YG Unpolished Full Set
description: The Rolex John Mayer Daytona reference 116508 is one of the most sought after watches on the planet. Nicknamed for the 2nd appearance on HODINKEE Talking Watches, this Rolex Daytona reference 116508 with green anniversary dial would take on the rockstar nickname "John Mayer". Despite being a departure from the Daytona’s utility-based nature, this model has become admired amongst a range of Rolex collectors and has been spotted on many other notable wrists including Jonah Hill’s. It has recently been discontinued. The case is made of 18k yellow gold. The watch is in great overall condition and is unpolished. The watch comes full set from the original owner. It was sold in 2023. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Sea-Dweller Reference 126600 As New Full Set
description: This Sea-Dweller reference 126600 is an awesome modern watch. The case is 43mm in diameter. The single line of red text is reminiscent of the original “Single Red” Sea-Dwellers that can be worth as much as $1 million. The watch is as new. The watch comes as a “full set” with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It was originally sold in 2022.

title: Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Green Dial 15510ST.OO.1320ST.04 Full Set Unpolished
description: The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is one of the most iconic and sought after sports watches on the planet. Designed by Gerald Genta, collectors have become enamored with this important timepiece from AP. This Audemers Piguet Royal Oak reference 15510ST is an incredible stainless steel sports watch. The case measures 41mm in diameter. The dial is a very special with a beautiful green “Grande Tapisserie” pattern. These models are among the most desired Audemars Piguet models. The watch comes full set with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It was sold in 2025.

title: Rolex Two-Tone Linen Dial Datejust Ref. 16233 Unpolished w/ Papers and Hangtag
description: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel ’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This model 16233 has a champagne-linen dial set with 18K gold applied coronet and index markers, shielded by a sapphire crystal. The bracelet features a T clasp code dating to circa 1995. The watch is in incredibly well preserved condition and we believe the case and Jubilee bracelet are unpolished. The watch features the caseback sticker. The watch does not come with a box. It comes with papers, and a hangtag and was originally sold in 1994. We do not know the service history but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Patek Philippe Cloisonné Enamel World Time Ref 5231J-001 in 18k YG Unpolished Full Set Like New
description: This Patek Philippe World Time reference 5231J-001 is an incredibly special timepiece. The dial is cloisonné Grand Feu enamel depicting the Americas, Europe, and Africa. The colors are tremendous in these dials and a beautiful scene each time you look at the time. The watch is powered by the calibre 240 HU - an ultra-thin self-winding movement with a 24-hour disc to set the time for any major city you travel to. The 18k yellow gold case is in like new condition and has only been worn a few times over the years. It features the classic World Time hour hand. The watch debuted in 2019 and is one of the most sophisticated Patek Philippe world time models. It measures 38.5mm in diameter, 47mm lug-to-lug and 10.25mm thick. The watch comes full set with box and papers. It was originally sold in December 2021.

title: Movado M95 Panda Chronograph Ref 19068 on Bracelet Unpolished
description: The Movado M95 chronographs are incredible timepieces not only for their movements, but also the cases and dials. This is an attractive example with a beautiful panda dial. The case measures 35mm in diameter. The case was produced by Taubert Frères (formerly François Borgel). It is paired on a Movado beads of rice bracelet. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. FIRM

title: Rolex Blue Mosaic Datejust Ref 1600 Unpolished
description: Here is your chance (but instead with a date added and with applied hour markers instead of 3, 6, and 9 luminous numerals)! It is a beautiful fiction, but a dream watch nonetheless. This Rolex Datejust reference 1600 is a spectacular watch with at the incredible blue mosaic (or shantung) dial. The dial was added later. The watch comes with its original luminous silver dial and handset. The case appears to be unpolished and the watch dates to circa 1971 with a 2.7M serial number. The watch measures 36mm in diameter. It is pared on a Rolex Oyster 7206 bracelet with 58 end links produced in 1969. It is one of the most attractive Datejusts out there. You never find unpolished reference 1600s, so this is a very exciting watch to wear and enjoy. It does not come with box and papers. It was recently serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex Tropical Non-Luminous Datejust 1601
description: The Simple Vintage Rolex Datejust In Steel ’. I’m constantly amazed by the quality and differentiations of the dials that Rolex used in vintage Datejusts over the decades. I have found the Rolex Datejust to be one of the most versatile watches a person can have. This is an incredibly attractive Rolex Datejust reference 1601 with the early fine fluted bezel in white gold. The dial is stunning with a chocolate tropical color and gilt lettering. The watch is also a rare non-luminous watch with the stunning alpha hands found in early Datejust models. The watch dates to circa 1961 and features a 702,XXX serial. The case measures 36mm in diameter and is paired on a Rolex Jubilee bracelet and 55 end links with a 1960 date code. The stainless steel case has been lightly polished. Finding a tropical dial Datejust that is so beautiful and natural is very difficult, particularly a non-luminous example. For those looking for an incredible Datejust, this is a special timepiece. It does not come with box and papers. We do not know the service history of this watch. It recently passed a pressure test of 5 bar.

title: Tudor No Date Submariner Ref 7016/0 Unpolished
description: This Tudor Submariner is a fantastic sports. The watch was made in circa 1970 with a 741,XXX serial. The case appears to be unpolished. The luminous material at 4,7, and 8 is slightly lighter. From our experience with the shield logo Tudor 7016/0s, the hands never really match the dial lume and we are not sure why. Nonetheless, the hands are possibly relumed. It features the original MK 3 bezel insert. It was tested to be water resistant to 5 bars. It does not come with box and papers. It is the epitome of the classic Tudor Submariner and is a rare no-date model. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Patek Philippe Aquanaut Reference 5968G-010 in 18K WG Unpolished Full Set
description: The Patek Philippe Aquanaut is one of the great contemporary sports watches. This Patek Philippe Aquanaut reference 5968G-001 is a modern classic with the green dial and strap. The strap is very long, so it will likely require the next owner to cut it down. At 42.2mm in diameter, it has fantastic proportions. The case is white gold. The case is unpolished. It has some wear. The watch is powered by the CH 28-520 C and has a flyback chronograph. It comes with box and papers from the original owner. It was purchased in 2025.

title: Rolex Patrizzi Dial Zenith Daytona Reference 16520
description: The adorned “Zenith Daytonas” are certainly among the highly desired neo-vintage Rolex models. Zenthi Daytonas were produced from circa 1988 until 2000. It is powered by an automatic Calibre 4030 made by Zenith. The case is lightly polished. It measures 40mm in diameter (42.5 including crown) and 47mm lug to lug. The outer ring of the registers have developed a very attractive beige color. Rolex register rings on Daytonas with black dials from the early through middle 1990s would often develop some darkening to the rings. This effect is often given the nickname “Patrizzi” after the legendary auctioneer Osvaldo Patrizzi, who reportedly first identified this patina. It features a T serial and dates to circa 1996. The bracelet code is V5 dating to 1996. The watch does not come with box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Patek Philippe Pocket Watch in 18K YG Unpolished
description: This Patek Philippe pocket watch is an extremely beautiful timepiece dating to the early 20th Century. The enamel dial - which is absolutely gorgeous. The case measures 47mm in diameter. It appears to be unpolished. The caseback features a beautiful Art Deco engraving of initials. The movement is signed Patek Philippe Shreve, Crump & Low - a prestigious Boston retailer of Patek Philippe. To learn more about Patek Philippe pocket watches, read Charlie Dunne’s article ‘ Why Your Perspective Should Change About Patek Philippe Pocket Watches ’. The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. We have ordered an Extract from the Archives.

title: Rolex Red Submariner Ref. 1680 Unpolished
description: This Rolex reference 1680 “Red Sub” is a great ‘date’ Submariner in stainless steel. The watch features a 3.03M serial number and dates to circa 1972. It is paired with a reference 9315 Rolex folded link Oyster bracelet dating to 1972. It comes with the correct 280 end-links. We believe it to be original to the watch. The dial is attractive and features Submariner in red writing along with tritium luminous plots that have turned warm with patina. The hands have been relumed. The case appears unpolished. The lug-to-lug length is approximately 47mm and it measures 40mm in diameter excluding the crown. The watch does not come with a box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well. The watch recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and corresponding article.

title: Rolex Submariner Reference 126610LN Unpolished Box & Papers
description: The Rolex Submariner is one of the most versatile and adorned wristwatches. The case measures 41mm in diameter. It appears to be unpolished. This particular example was purchased in 2023. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, you can watch my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and read the corresponding article. The watch comes “full set” with box and papers from the original owner. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar. It is running well.

title: Heuer Dato Carrera Reference 3147N Indianapolis Motor Speedway Indy 500
description: The Heuer Carrera is one of the legendary chronograph designs of the 20th century. Featuring stark angled lugs that were a product of the architecture of the 1960s, the present watch is notably a “first execution” and features an unpolished case. Finding Carreras from the 1960s with in great condition is extremely difficult. The reference 3147 was the first Heuer Carrera with date and this is a 3147N with the N meaning “Noir” indicating a black dial. The movement is a Landeron 148 with date and the case back is thicker on these to accommodate the thicker movement with the date. The present example is a grail Heuer that has been in a private collection for approximately 30 years. It features the Indianapolis Motor Speedway logo and likely was made for Tony Hulman who ordered some Heuer watches and stopwatches for use at the track and for friends. An Autavia model with this dial sold at Christie’s for nearly $200,000 back in 2017 . You can read more about the origins of the Carrera in the in-depth article that I wrote for HODINKEE back in 2013 . The case diameter is 36mm with a lug width of 18mm. The case has been lightly polished, but is strong. This is a grail watch and perhaps a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity for Heuer and racing enthusiasts. We do not know the service history, but the watch is running well. It does not come with a box or papers.

title: Robin Williams' Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Reference 270.8.54 in Full Set Unpolished
description: This Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso Dual Time reference 270.8.54 is a great stainless steel wrist watch with two dials. The watch has an incredible provenance and was owned by the comedian and actor Robin Williams. Robin Williams is regarded as one of the most important and legendary comedians who stood out for his incredible stage presence, energy, accents and impersonations. Williams was a generational talent for his dynamic on-screen talent in comedy roles, as well as dramas and thrillers. Williams won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor for his role in the 1997 film Good Will Hunting . He won a Golden Globe for Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy in the 1988 film Good Morning, Vietnam , as well Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy for Mrs. Doubtfire . Williams’ comedy career garnered immense praise from critics and his peers. He won a Grammy for Best Comedy Album on his albums Reality...What a Concept, Good Morning, Vietnam, A Night at the Met’ and Robin Williams Live - 2002. The watch was sold at Sotheby’s in 2018 at the Creating a Stage: The Collection of Marsha and Robin Williams auction. This watch is an incredibly sentimental opportunity for fans of the iconic actor/comedian. The white dial is time only with a small center seconds sub dial. The black dial also sub-dials for 24 hours. Both dials feature guilloché finishing. The stainless steel case measures approximately 26mm x 42mm. The watch comes as a “full set” with box and papers and dates to 2001. We do not know the service history, but it is running well.

title: Rolex Big Daytona Ref 6239 Unpolished
description: The Rolex Daytona reference 6239 is the original and earliest reference of the Daytona. This example features a “Big Daytona” logo where DAYTONA is large and underneath COSMOGRAPH on the dial. I really love the way the ROLEX / COSMOGRAPH / DAYTONA text lines up in descending order like a pyramid in shape. This example also has the original early “300” Mark 2 reference 6239 bezel, which differs from later examples by having 300 on the tachymeter bezel and “UNITS PER HOUR” between 12 and 2 o’clock, whereas later bezels have it by 3 o’clock. This is the second version of the steel bezel found on the reference 6239 and the earliest Mark 1 examples have a 275 and dashes rather than dots next to the numbers. The case features a 1.62 million serial number dating it to circa 1967. The case appears to be unpolished. This example was previously owned by a fighter pilot. He had great vision and purchased an amazing Rolex. It is paired on an extremely rare and original bracelet with 71N end links dating to 1967. Most dealers would sell this on a solid link bracelet from the 1980s (YUCK!). The watch does not come with box and papers. The watch features a new TrueDome D21 crystal. It was just serviced and it is running well.

title: TAG Heuer HODINKEE Skipper Limited Edition Full Set
description: The Skipper is one of the many incredible Heuer watches to feature a touch of vibrant design. Drawing inspiration from the iconic reference 7754, the Skipperara would re-emerge in June 2017 when HODINKEE introduced this limited edition to great fanfare. The watch swiftly sold out and since its release, the watch remains one of the most successful limited editions from both the media company and watchmaker. The Skipper is a tremendous watch for the summer, capturing a high-spirited look on just about any strap paring. The watch is powered by the TAG Heuer caliber 18, and is on view through the sapphire exhibition caseback. The dimensions are 39mm in diameter, 11mm tall, and the lug-width is 19mm. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch comes "full set" with box and papers. It comes with the three strap options. It was just serviced by TAG Heuer and is running well.

title: Rolex Non-Luminous "Wide Boy" Day-Date 1803 in 18K YG Unpolished
description: This Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 is a phenomenal gold watch. The case appears to be unpolished. It is made of 18k yello gold and measures 36mm in diameter. It features the rare non-luminous champagne “Wide Boy” dial. The watch dates to circa 1970 with a 2.4M serial. To learn more about these dials, read Charlie Dunne’s article “ The Rolex "Wide Boy" Datejust ”. The watch does not include box and papers. We do not know the service history but it is running well.

title: Rolex Submariner Ref. 5513 Maxi MK 5 Dial Unpolished
description: This Rolex Submariner reference 5513 is a fantatistic example. The watch features an 8.1M serial dating to circa 1984. The case appears to be unpolished. It is paired with a reference 93150 Oyster with an H clasp code bracelet dating to 1983. It has the correct 580 end-links. To learn more about the Rolex Submariner, dive into my ‘ Reference Points: Understanding The Rolex Submariner ’ video and corresponding article. This watch does not come with box and papers. It just was serviced and is running well. It recently passed a water resistance test of 5 bar.

title: Rolex 14K Two-Tone Datejust 16013 Unpolished
description: This two-tone Rolex Datejust reference 16013 is an attractive timepiece. It features an awesome champagne dial with great hour markers. It is a rare version of the 16013 with 14k gold accents instead of the standard 18k. The case measures 36mm in diameter. The case appears to be unpolished. The watch dates to circa 1982 with a 7.11M serial. The bracelet features a G date code dating to circa 1982. This watch does not include the box or papers. We do not know the service history, but it is running well. It recently passed a pressure test of 5 bar. This watch is available through J. Press.
